---
title: "Week 7 Milestone Factors"
author: "<img src='images/logo.png' width='50' /> <br> Dairy Cow Foot Doc"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  event:
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  zoo
  )

events_formatted_all <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_all_columns.parquet') |>
  select(source_file_path, ID, BDAT, FDAT, LACT, ARDAT, Event, Date, 
         Protocols, Remark, R, T, B)
         
solution <- readr::read_rds("data_milestones/solutions_week_07.rds")

# =========================================================================
# Survey commands
# =========================================================================

# create variables for inline code
# write_csv(events_formatted_all,"excel_excuses.csv")
# events_formatted_all
# names(events_formatted_all)
# names(solution)
# str(events_formatted_all)
# str(solution)
# unique(events_formatted_all$Event)
# names(solution_data)
# str(solution_data)
# unique(events_formatted_all$LACT)
# unique(solution_data$date_event)
# unique(solution$remark_letters1)
# unique(solution$protocols_letters1)


```

```{=html}
<!-- The objective of this milestone is to use the template report you created last week to create another report while learning how to call functions from other places.
Then you will learn the skills from this week to create a graph that takes data from text and date fields. You will use factor skills to order the graph properly to make your point cleaer.
A few notes:  1) I put the code on how to insert an image in the header in the yaml change it to use your logo. 2)Any data (including farm name) that is reported in the text should use an inline text code chunk -->
```

Run this chuck below. It will open the example report in your browser. You should delete this chunk in your final report

```{r}
#| label: open-html-r
#| eval: false

# 1. Define the file path
file_path <- "images/milestone_7.htm"

# 2. Open the browser
browseURL(file_path)
```

# Report for farm name

-   This report shows data from the past year from min date to max data

## Objective

Your objective is to recreate the solution dataset by doing the following with the raw_data table.

-   id_animal is a combination of source_file_path, ID, BDAT
-   Herd is extracted from the source_file_path extract the protocol data from the text fields
-   protocols1 and remark1 contain the first set of character strings before a non character string is found.
-   convert the exported data fields to real dates

## Your Code Creation

```{r}
#| label: your code

# put your code here to create the parsed variables. name it solution_data

# =========================================================================
# Data cleaning and conversion.  Create solution_data
# =========================================================================


solution_data <- events_formatted_all %>%
  mutate(
    # extract herd ID information and concatenate with ID and BDAT.
    id_animal = str_c(
      str_sub(source_file_path, start = 18, end = 53),
      ID,
      BDAT,
      sep = "_"
    ),
    # Convert BDAT to date_birth
    date_birth = if_else(BDAT == "00000000", NA, mdy(BDAT)),
    # Convert FDAT to date_fresh
    date_fresh = if_else(FDAT == "00000000", NA, mdy(FDAT)),
    # Convert ARDAT to date_archived
    date_archived = if_else(ARDAT == "00000000", NA, mdy(ARDAT)),
    # Convert Date to date_event
    date_event = if_else(Date == "00000000", NA, mdy(Date)),
    # Convert LACT to numeric lact_number
    lact_number = as.numeric(LACT),
    # Extract leading letters from Remark (skip non-letters at start)
    remark_letters1 = str_extract(
      str_remove(Remark, "^[^A-Za-z]+"),
      "^[A-Za-z]+"
    ),
    # Extract leading letters from Protocols (skip non-letters at start)
    protocols_letters1 = str_extract(
      str_remove(Protocols, "^[^A-Za-z]+"),
      "^[A-Za-z]+"
    ),
    # Create location_event by taking first 5 chars of 'id_animal' and prefixing with "Herd "
    location_event = str_c("Herd ", str_sub(id_animal, start = 1, end = 5))
  ) %>%
  # rename Event -> event
  rename(event = Event) %>%
  # select the columns of interest to complete 'solution_data'
  select(
    id_animal,
    date_birth,
    date_fresh,
    lact_number,
    date_archived,
    date_event,
    event,
    remark_letters1,
    protocols_letters1,
    R,
    T,
    B,
    location_event
  )




```

```{r}
#| label: create-solution-data

# waldo::compare(solution_data, solution)



```

### Create DIM Categories

Then on the new data table.

-   Create a dim_cat that splits DIM into 6 categories.
-   Order this variable in the correct order so if you were to graph it the categories would make sense and would not be in numerical or alphabetical order

```{r}
#| label: dim-cat-order
#| echo: true

solution_data <- solution_data %>%
  mutate(
    # calculate difference in days
    dim_event = as.numeric(difftime(date_event, date_fresh, units = "days")),
    
    # categorize dim_event into bins
    dim_cat = case_when(
      dim_event >= 270 ~ ">270",
      dim_event >= 211 & dim_event <= 270 ~ "211-270",
      dim_event >= 151 & dim_event <= 210 ~ "151-210",
      dim_event >= 91 & dim_event <= 150  ~ "91-150",
      dim_event >= 30 & dim_event <= 90   ~ "30-90",
      dim_event >= 0  & dim_event <= 29   ~ "<30",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_   # catch missing values
    ),
    
    # categorize lact_number into bins
    lact_grp5plus = case_when(
      lact_number >= 5   ~ "LGRP(5+)",
      lact_number == 4   ~ "LGRP(4)",
      lact_number == 3   ~ "LGRP(3)",
      lact_number == 2   ~ "LGRP(2)",
      lact_number == 1   ~ "LGRP(1)",
      lact_number == 0   ~ "LGRP(0)",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_   # catch missing values
    )
  ) %>%
  # Sort the dataframe by these columns
  arrange(id_animal, lact_number, event, date_event) %>%
  # Add sequential counter for events per cow/lactation/event type
  group_by(id_animal, lact_number, event) %>%
  mutate(seq = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()





```

### Import Function

Then import this function from Github This function will create new variables for LAME and TRIM remarks in your data set that now has clean protocol and remark fields and an associated dim_cat variable. The function is called fxn_code_lesions() and it takes 3 input variables event_var, protocol_var, remark_var.

```{r}
#| label: import-functions
#| echo: true

raw_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dairy-Cow-Foot-Doc/os_functions/main/fxn_code_lesions.R"

source(raw_url)

```

### Create New Variables

Then use this function to create new variables for the lesions in your data set.

```{r}
#| label: add-new-variables
#| echo: true

# put your code for recoding the variables here

# Apply the function to solution_data
solution_data <- fxn_code_lesions(
  solution_data,
  event_var = event,
  protocol_var = protocols_letters1,
  remark_var = remark_letters1
)

# names(solution_data)

# =========================================================================
# Summarize the coded lesions for LAME & TRIM, for 2024-01-01 forward
# =========================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

# columns to summarize
cols_to_summarize <- c(
  "trimonly", "sole_ulcer", "wld", "toe_ulcer", "thin", "toe",
  "sole_fracture", "dd", "footrot", "hemorrhage", "axial", "cork",
  "injury", "other", "inf", "noninf", "lesion"
)

# restrict dataset first
filtered_data <- solution_data %>%
  filter(
    event %in% c("TRIM", "LAME"),
    date_event >= as.Date("2024-01-01")
  )

# build summary grid
summary_grid <- map_dfr(cols_to_summarize, function(col) {
  tibble(
    column = col,
    count_0  = sum(filtered_data[[col]] == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    count_1  = sum(filtered_data[[col]] == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    count_NA = sum(is.na(filtered_data[[col]]))
  )
})

summary_grid



```

## Plot Data

-   Summarize this new data for the past 12 months to create a graph that looks similar to this

```{r}
#| label: fig-example_lame
#| fig-cap: "Lameness Lesions by DIM Category"
#| echo: true



  
# =========================================================================
# Aggregate lesion count, ALL Cases, ALL Lactations
# =========================================================================
  
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(forcats)

# Define lesion columns
all_lesions <- c("dd", "footrot", "wld", "sole_ulcer", "injury", 
                 "cork", "other", "hemorrhage", "sole_fracture", 
                 "toe_ulcer", "thin", "inf", "noninf", "toe", 
                 "axial", "lesion")

# Filter data: only 'LAME' events and date >= 2024-01-01
filtered_data <- solution_data %>%
  filter(event == "LAME", date_event >= as.Date("2024-01-01"))

# Pivot lesion columns into long format to facilitate count
lesion_long <- filtered_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(all_lesions), 
               names_to = "lesion", 
               values_to = "count") %>%
  filter(count > 0, !is.na(dim_cat))   # Exclude counts = 0 and NA categories

# Summarize counts by lesion and category
lesion_summary <- lesion_long %>%
  group_by(lesion, dim_cat) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Order lesions by total counts (descending)
lesion_order <- lesion_summary %>%
  group_by(lesion) %>%
  summarise(grand_total = sum(total)) %>%
  arrange(desc(grand_total)) %>%
  pull(lesion)

# Manually define the order of categories
dim_cat_order <- c("<30", "30-90", "91-150", "151-210", "211-270", ">270")

# Convert dim_cat to an ordered factor (legend order you want)
lesion_summary <- lesion_summary %>%
  mutate(dim_cat = factor(dim_cat, levels = dim_cat_order))

# Determine the total count 'max_total' to set the dynamic Y-axis
max_total <- lesion_summary %>%
  group_by(lesion) %>%
  summarise(grand_total = sum(total)) %>%
  summarise(max_total = max(grand_total)) %>%
  pull(max_total)

# Define mapping of lesion codes to descriptive labels
lesion_labels <- c(
  "dd" = "Digital Dermatitis",
  "footrot" = "Footrot",
  "wld" = "White Line Disease",
  "sole_ulcer" = "Sole Ulcer",
  "hemorrhage" = "Hemorrhage",
  "sole_fracture" = "Sole Fracture",
  "toe_ulcer" = "Toe Ulcer",
  "thin" = "Thin Soles",
  "cork" = "Corkscrew Claw",
  "injury" = "Injury",
  "inf" = "Infectious",
  "noninf" = "Non-Infectious",
  "toe" = "Toe",
  "axial" = "Axial",
  "other" = "Other Lesions",
  "lesion" = "Lesion Present"
)

# Plot: reverse stacking in bars, keep legend order
ggplot(lesion_summary, aes(x = factor(lesion, levels = rev(lesion_order)), 
                           y = total, 
                           fill = fct_rev(dim_cat))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black") +  # Outline bar in black
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlGnBu") +  # ColorBrewer code
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max_total, 250)) +  # dynamic set off 'max_total'
  scale_x_discrete(labels = lesion_labels) +  # descriptive labels for lesions
  labs(title = "Lesion Counts by Category (LAME Events, 2024+)",
       caption = "All cases, All Lactations",
       x = "Lesion Type",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Category") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))









  


```

# Extension

For this week extension continue to create standard things would want to report

Ideas (or hints for next week include)

start doing math with dates so we can calculate time to event data (cull/repeat)
